title: referrals — model relationships
description: Five owned entities. Every row is tenant-scoped (cascades on tenant delete). ReferralProgram is the aggregate root; ReferralCode belongs to a Program; Referral belongs to a Program + optional Code; ReferralReward belongs to a Referral; ReferralFraudFlag belongs to a Referral. Every outbound FK stays same-tenant per tenancy-columns.md §5. NO application_id / organization_id / region_id / branch_id / scope_node_id on any owned row — all cascade through tenant_id.

internal.description: A program can distribute many codes (per-user codes + campaign codes + opaque codes).
RESTRICT on delete. Codes anchor to their program; a paused/archived program keeps its codes for audit + already-issued referrals.
A program tracks many referral instances over its lifetime.
RESTRICT on delete. Referrals retain 7 years for financial audit; program cannot be hard-deleted while referrals exist.
One code may be used by many referred users (subject to usage_cap).
Nullable — a referral tracked purely via attribution (click carrying utm parameters but no code) has no code.
1..2 rewards per referral (single-sided = 1 for referrer; double-sided = 2, one per side).
RESTRICT on delete. Rewards outlive referrals for financial audit.
Zero or many fraud flags per referral. A single referral can accumulate flags from multiple detection passes (claim-time + trigger-time).
RESTRICT on delete. Flags retain 7 years for fraud audit.

outbound.description: Every program is tenant-scoped per tenancy-columns.md §3.
Owner FK for user_scoped codes only. Null for campaign / opaque codes. RESTRICT: user hard-delete cannot orphan their code (redaction handled by RedactReferralDataOnUserErasure hook).
Polymorphic referrer. Concrete types: user::User (usr_), leads::AnonymousLead (lea_ — future). RESTRICT for audit-integrity — a referrer's hard-delete cannot orphan reward audit history.
Polymorphic referred. Nullable — a claim (link clicked) may not yet resolve to a User/Lead. Concrete types: user::User, leads::Lead.
Polymorphic recipient. Concrete types: user::User, leads::Lead (rare — some programs reward pre-signup leads). RESTRICT for financial-record integrity.
Wave 4 — the Finance credit created when this reward materialised. Nullable until MaterializeVestedRewardsJob successfully calls Finance.
The admin who reviewed the flag. Nullable while flag is unreviewed.

inbound.description: Downstream modules attach in later waves:
examples: finance::Credit (Wave 4) — every materialised ReferralReward creates a Credit row that references the reward via reward_id + reward_source='referral'.
marketing::MarketingEvent (Wave 5) — ReferralSignedUp + ReferralVested fire marketing events; the referral_id is captured on the event row for revenue attribution.
attribution::Attribution (Wave 5, already shipped) — Referral.attribution_snapshot is captured from AttributionSnapshotBuilder at claim time.
inbound.note: No inbound relations in Wave 5 beyond the internal set — this module is a growth-tier terminal aggregate.

morphs.note: Wave 5 ships user only; leads::AnonymousLead is a placeholder for Wave 6 lead-capture module.
Wave 5 ships user only. Both nullable during the claim/click stage before user resolution.

notes: Referral does NOT carry `application_id`. Referrals cascade through the tenant's application per tenancy-columns.md §2. Cross-application referrals are non-goals — a Sports-app referrer cannot refer to Marketplace-app.
ReferralReward does NOT carry `referral_program_id` directly — it cascades through `referral_id -> program_id`. Denormalisation deferred until proven necessary for query planning.

The composite unique index on (tenant_id, referral_program_id, referrer_id, referred_id) partial WHERE deleted_at IS NULL enforces one referral per program per referrer-referred pair — a referred user cannot be re-referred by the same referrer for the same program.

ReferralFraudFlag can have MULTIPLE rows per Referral (one per detection pass + flag type). A referral flagged for `self_ip_match` at claim time may accumulate a `velocity_exceeded` flag at trigger time.
polymorphic morphTo on referrer/referred/recipient uses `stackra/foundation`'s HasPolyRelation trait — refuses cross-tenant morph resolution at write time.
